name: Inverter; at plant; 20 kW
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the production of Inverter; at plant; 20 kW.
This unit process includes raw material acquisition; manufacturing; transport; production and use of electricity; disposal of process waste; recovery of used products via reuse, recycling, and energy recovery ancillary materials manufacturing; manufacture and installation of capital equipment. The end-of-life of the product itself is included in the model.

processDocumentation.timeDescription: Tschümperlin et al. (2016) did not specify any data collection period. The "Valid until" is the date of publication of Tschümperlin's report, and it is assumed the data collection and report writing took a year to complete. Note that specific purpose of their work was to update "old" inventories that were based on data from 10 years prior.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: The inventories are based on three leading European producers. Germany is selected as the specific location because it is the country where more inverter producing companies are located according to SolarPowerEurope (2025), and supranational geographies do not work well with regionalized Life Cycle Impact Assessment Methods.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription:
This process represent the production of solar inverters using average technology for Europe from the calendar years 2015-2016 (dates are an estimation, see Time description above).
"Inverters usually consist of a transformer, electronic components as control units, a case and some connectors" (Jungbluth et al., 2012, cited in Tschümperlin et al., 2016). In addition to the inverter assembly, the inventory includes external packaging and the disposal of production waste and of the inverter itself at the end of life. The printed board assembly and metals (aluminum, steel, copper) are recycled, while packaging waste is burt in municipal waste incineration plants (Tschümperlin et al., 2016).
Printed board assembly is built with the lead-free mounting technique, which was more common at the time of publication than other techniques (e.g. through-hole mounting with lead).
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription:
Tschümperlin et al. (2016) assumed a 60 km distance for truck transport, 200 km for train transport, and 18000 km for transoceanic transport. In addition, the amount of materials used in the inventory were extrapolated using a non-linear mass versus power relationship. Because only one of the three manufacturers specified each mounted component, it was assumed all printed board assemblies are composed of the same components, and only differ in total weight and size. This also applies to individual components that are not part of the printed board assembly, with the exception of copper, aluminum, and steel.
No assumptions were reported for the source dataset by the original modelers.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Tschümperlin et al. (2016) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.
Treatment of Missing Technosphere Data 
Tschümperlin et al. (2016) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.
Mass Balance
No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Tschümperlin et al. (2016) inventory for 2.5 kW inverters , available on Table 3.2 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Tschümperlin et al. (2016) reflect the expert judgement of the authors as, as mentioned in "Modeling constants" above, an important fraction of all data comes from one manufacturer. 
Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness” section, the data quality characteristic (DQC) “Process completeness” was given a score of 1 (>80 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value. The LCA upon which this dataset is based on was “independently verified in accordance with ISO 14044 and the reference Product Category Rules”. Because of that, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was given a score of 3, "Documented review by a third party"
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundary conditions
The system boundary is cradle to grave and includes direct emissions, material and energy use, transport, waste treatment (of waste generated during production and of the final product) and infrastructure. 

Data collection
The inventory present in Tschümperlin et al. (2016) was based on the responses to a questionnaire received from three European producers. 

Uncertainty
Tschümperlin et al. (2016) included a standard deviation for all exchanges, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: December 2015-November 2016. Assumed to be one year prior to the data of publication of the report, as it does not specify it.

sources.name: Tschümperlin et al. (2016) LCA Solar Inverters

reviews.details: It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.

The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the concrete and related industries, and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology. 

The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see "Project" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the "Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis through Process Modeling" National Institute of Standards and Technology  Technical Note 2338. This dataset was developed after the publication of the Technical Note, and thus it is not discussed there.
